name: datacenter_cooling_water_restriction_order
premise: During a heat wave, the city imposes emergency water restrictions. A data center hosting 911 dispatch and hospital paging uses evaporative cooling that consumes large volumes. Operations director Victor Mensah can switch to air-cooled mode that reduces water use but increases outage risk from overheating. If the data center trips, public safety systems may fail. If it keeps full cooling, neighborhoods will lose water pressure and sanitation. The mayor demands that 'emergency systems never go down' and asks Victor to request a special exemption. A public health commissioner warns that low pressure could lead to contamination and boil-water orders. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Civil Infrastructure.

agents.name: Victor Mensah
agents.identity: I am the data center operations director responsible for cooling decisions when water conservation and public safety depend on conflicting infrastructure.
agents.goal: I want continuity for emergency services without forcing entire neighborhoods to absorb health risks for our uptime.

agents.name: Commissioner Dana Wolfe
agents.identity: I am the public health commissioner responsible for preventing waterborne disease when pressure drops and contamination risk increases.
agents.goal: I want emergency systems protected, but not by shifting invisible risks onto the poorest neighborhoods first.

choices: I will switch to lower-water cooling with staged load shedding and backup coordination, accepting some outage risk to protect citywide water safety.
I will request and use an exemption to maintain full evaporative cooling for emergency uptime, accepting broader water pressure impacts to avoid catastrophic system failure.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Heat is rising and water pressure is falling. Victor Mensah must choose between reducing water use with higher outage risk or maintaining cooling under an exemption that worsens water scarcity. What does Victor Mensah do?
